task_name: Add JWT Authentication System to REST API

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the complete implementation plan:

---

## Plan Summary

### Files to create (2 new)

**`src/middleware/auth.ts`**
- Exports `AuthRequest` interface (extends `Request`, adds `user: { id: number; username: string }`)
- Exports `authenticate` middleware: reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, calls `jwt.verify()` in try/catch, sets `(req as AuthRequest).user`, returns 401 on failure

**`src/routes/auth.ts`**
- `hashPassword()` using Node's built-in `createHash("sha256")` from `node:crypto`
- `POST /register` → validates fields (400), checks duplicate username (409), hashes password, creates user, returns `{ token, user: { id, username, email } }` (no password field)
- `POST /login` → verifies credentials against hash, returns `{ token }`

### Files to modify (3 existing)

**`src/app.ts`** — add `app.use("/auth", authRouter)`

**`src/routes/posts.ts`** — add `authenticate` to POST/PUT/DELETE:
- POST: use `(req as AuthRequest).user.id` instead of `req.body.userId || 1`
- PUT + DELETE: add ownership check → 403 if `post.userId !== req.user.id`

**`src/routes/comments.ts`** — same pattern for POST/PUT/DELETE comments

### Key design decisions

| Topic | Decision |
|-------|----------|
| `req.user` typing | Export `AuthRequest` from middleware, cast `req as AuthRequest` in handlers (avoids fragile global namespace augmentation under Node16) |
| JWT import | `import jwt from "jsonwebtoken"` — default only; named imports fail at runtime for this CJS package under ESM |
| Seed users | Leave plaintext passwords untouched — no test logs in as alice/bob/charlie, and changing would break `posts.test.ts` |
| All imports | Must use `.js` extensions (Node16 module resolution requirement) |

### Verification
```bash
npm test   # all tests in posts, comments, and auth test files should pass
```
